course.title: Independent Product Studio I: Discover, Build, Validate, and Defend
course.description: Advanced learners ready to retrieve prior programming and web foundations while independently discovering, building, validating, releasing, and defending a small but real stakeholder product. This original, research-backed path uses repeated retrieval, cumulative artifacts, changed-case labs, stakeholder projects, and a performance exam.
outcomes: Demonstrate a complete independent product cycle from readiness audit and ethical discovery through outcomes, prototypes, vertical slices, reproducible repository, architecture, cumulative implementation, inclusive experience, trust evidence, release, recovery, and defense through an independently verified stakeholder artifact.

Demonstrate original guided, faded, debugging, independent, retrieval, assessment, and transfer work that grades affected-user behavior and evidence rather than code shape through an independently verified stakeholder artifact.
Demonstrate five materially different public-benefit milestones and one unfamiliar cumulative product defense through an independently verified stakeholder artifact.
sharedRequirements: Every activity retrieves the prior problem, stakeholder, affected-user, accessibility, security, privacy, evidence, revision, test, operation, recovery, contribution, and non-claim boundaries before adding complexity.

Browser labs analyze deterministic original evidence manifests only. They never execute learner shell commands, contact research participants or external services, deploy software, access secrets, or claim live accessibility, security, privacy, load, recovery, legal, or production behavior.

Passing work requires a prediction, traceable artifact, deterministic verification, changed and rejected cases, causal diagnosis, repair evidence, affected-user or stakeholder evidence where appropriate, explicit limitations, and an accountable owner.

competencies.statement: Map prior competencies to observable behaviors, dated artifacts, evidence strength, and unresolved gaps before selecting a product.
misconceptions: Course completion badges prove every underlying skill remains retrievable.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Map prior competencies to observable behaviors, dated artifacts, evidence strength, and unresolved gaps before selecting a product.

competencies.statement: Distinguish reusable evidence, practice residue, generated output, sensitive material, and unsupported claims in an existing work sample.
misconceptions: Anything committed to a repository is suitable portfolio evidence.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Distinguish reusable evidence, practice residue, generated output, sensitive material, and unsupported claims in an existing work sample.

competencies.statement: Reproduce one prior behavior under a changed input, environment, or constraint and retain its exact pass or failure evidence.
misconceptions: Repeating the original happy path demonstrates durable mastery.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Reproduce one prior behavior under a changed input, environment, or constraint and retain its exact pass or failure evidence.

competencies.statement: Convert a readiness gap into a bounded retrieval task with acceptance evidence and a stop rule.
misconceptions: The project should silently teach every missing prerequisite while scope grows.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Convert a readiness gap into a bounded retrieval task with acceptance evidence and a stop rule.

competencies.statement: Create a revision-bound ledger linking each claim to artifact, verification, changed case, limitation, and owner.
misconceptions: A polished narrative can replace traceable evidence.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Create a revision-bound ledger linking each claim to artifact, verification, changed case, limitation, and owner.

competencies.statement: Separate the requested feature from the user need, stakeholder decision, current behavior, and desired outcome.
misconceptions: A stakeholder solution request is already a validated problem statement.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Separate the requested feature from the user need, stakeholder decision, current behavior, and desired outcome.

competencies.statement: Observe or reconstruct the current workflow with roles, triggers, inputs, handoffs, delays, exceptions, and consequences.
misconceptions: A generic persona provides enough evidence about real work.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Observe or reconstruct the current workflow with roles, triggers, inputs, handoffs, delays, exceptions, and consequences.

competencies.statement: Compare direct observation, interview, artifact, analytics, support, market, and assumption evidence for relevance and bias.
misconceptions: All evidence sources deserve equal confidence.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Compare direct observation, interview, artifact, analytics, support, market, and assumption evidence for relevance and bias.

competencies.statement: Estimate who encounters the problem, how often, with what severity, and under which boundary conditions.
misconceptions: A vivid anecdote establishes population-level importance.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Estimate who encounters the problem, how often, with what severity, and under which boundary conditions.

competencies.statement: Record open questions and postpone feature commitment until the problem and constraints meet an explicit evidence threshold.
misconceptions: Discovery is successful only when it approves the original build idea.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Record open questions and postpone feature commitment until the problem and constraints meet an explicit evidence threshold.

competencies.statement: Frame neutral research questions around behavior, context, barriers, and decisions instead of selling a preferred solution.
misconceptions: Asking whether users like the idea measures authentic need.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Frame neutral research questions around behavior, context, barriers, and decisions instead of selling a preferred solution.

competencies.statement: Recruit relevant variation in ability, device, language, connectivity, role, and problem experience without claiming representativeness.
misconceptions: The easiest available participants adequately represent affected users.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Recruit relevant variation in ability, device, language, connectivity, role, and problem experience without claiming representativeness.

competencies.statement: Explain purpose, recording, storage, withdrawal, incentives, and power relationships in accessible consent before participation.
misconceptions: A checked box makes any collection ethically acceptable.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Explain purpose, recording, storage, withdrawal, incentives, and power relationships in accessible consent before participation.

competencies.statement: Collect only decision-necessary research data with retention, access, redaction, deletion, and breach handling.
misconceptions: More raw participant data always produces better insight.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Collect only decision-necessary research data with retention, access, redaction, deletion, and breach handling.

competencies.statement: Synthesize patterns with source links, counterevidence, minority needs, uncertainty, and quotations short enough to protect identity.
misconceptions: A majority theme permits discarding contradictory or accessibility-specific evidence.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Synthesize patterns with source links, counterevidence, minority needs, uncertainty, and quotations short enough to protect identity.

competencies.statement: Connect product capability to user behavior, user outcome, stakeholder outcome, and public consequence without assuming causality.
misconceptions: Shipping a capability directly proves the intended real-world outcome.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Connect product capability to user behavior, user outcome, stakeholder outcome, and public consequence without assuming causality.

competencies.statement: Define a baseline, observable leading and lagging measures, collection method, comparison window, and uncertainty.
misconceptions: A target is meaningful without a baseline or measurement plan.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Define a baseline, observable leading and lagging measures, collection method, comparison window, and uncertainty.

competencies.statement: Pair success measures with accessibility, equity, privacy, security, reliability, and burden guardrails.
misconceptions: Optimizing the primary metric cannot make the product worse overall.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Pair success measures with accessibility, equity, privacy, security, reliability, and burden guardrails.

competencies.statement: State non-goals, excluded users or cases, unsupported claims, and the evidence required to expand scope.
misconceptions: Omitting a feature from the plan communicates a stable non-goal.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: State non-goals, excluded users or cases, unsupported claims, and the evidence required to expand scope.

competencies.statement: Define evidence thresholds that trigger continue, revise, pause, or stop decisions before sunk cost grows.
misconceptions: Stopping after negative evidence means the project failed.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Define evidence thresholds that trigger continue, revise, pause, or stop decisions before sunk cost grows.

competencies.statement: Express desirable, usable, feasible, viable, ethical, accessible, secure, and operable assumptions as falsifiable claims.
misconceptions: A risk list that names topics without predictions is testable.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Express desirable, usable, feasible, viable, ethical, accessible, secure, and operable assumptions as falsifiable claims.

competencies.statement: Prioritize assumptions by uncertainty, consequence, dependency, and cheapest discriminating evidence.
misconceptions: The technically hardest feature is always the riskiest assumption.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Prioritize assumptions by uncertainty, consequence, dependency, and cheapest discriminating evidence.

competencies.statement: Choose paper, clickable, coded, data, integration, or operational prototype fidelity to answer one question without production overinvestment.
misconceptions: Higher fidelity always creates more valid evidence.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Choose paper, clickable, coded, data, integration, or operational prototype fidelity to answer one question without production overinvestment.

competencies.statement: Run a protocol with success, failure, observation, accessibility accommodation, and interpretation criteria set before the session.
misconceptions: Any positive participant comment validates the prototype.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Run a protocol with success, failure, observation, accessibility accommodation, and interpretation criteria set before the session.

competencies.statement: Separate disposable exploration from production code and record what can and cannot transfer after the experiment.
misconceptions: A prototype that appears to work should become the production foundation.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Separate disposable exploration from production code and record what can and cannot transfer after the experiment.

competencies.statement: Write a bounded user scenario with trigger, actor, need, outcome, constraints, and observable acceptance evidence.
misconceptions: A component or database task is automatically a user story.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Write a bounded user scenario with trigger, actor, need, outcome, constraints, and observable acceptance evidence.

competencies.statement: Slice work across interface, behavior, data, verification, accessibility, security, and operations to produce one end-to-end learning signal.
misconceptions: Completing each architecture layer separately reduces integration risk.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Slice work across interface, behavior, data, verification, accessibility, security, and operations to produce one end-to-end learning signal.

competencies.statement: Order slices by prerequisite knowledge, risk retirement, feedback value, and reversible commitment.
misconceptions: Business priority alone determines pedagogical and technical order.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Order slices by prerequisite knowledge, risk retirement, feedback value, and reversible commitment.

competencies.statement: Define done as behavior plus changed-case tests, accessibility review, security checks, documentation, deployment, and recovery evidence.
misconceptions: A merged implementation means the slice is done.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Define done as behavior plus changed-case tests, accessibility review, security checks, documentation, deployment, and recovery evidence.

competencies.statement: Protect the outcome by trading feature breadth, fidelity, automation, and polish while preserving non-negotiable quality boundaries.
misconceptions: Fixed time can be met by temporarily dropping accessibility or testing.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Protect the outcome by trading feature breadth, fidelity, automation, and polish while preserving non-negotiable quality boundaries.

competencies.statement: Establish purpose, setup, architecture, commands, contribution, conduct, support, security, license, and ownership documentation.
misconceptions: A README installation snippet is a complete repository contract.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Establish purpose, setup, architecture, commands, contribution, conduct, support, security, license, and ownership documentation.

competencies.statement: Pin supported runtime, package manager, compiler, formatter, linter, type checker, test runner, and build behavior with compatibility evidence.
misconceptions: Latest floating tools keep the repository modern without risk.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Pin supported runtime, package manager, compiler, formatter, linter, type checker, test runner, and build behavior with compatibility evidence.

competencies.statement: Evaluate dependency need, maintenance, provenance, license, advisories, transitive cost, API stability, and replacement or removal path.
misconceptions: A popular package is automatically safe and appropriate.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Evaluate dependency need, maintenance, provenance, license, advisories, transitive cost, API stability, and replacement or removal path.

competencies.statement: Separate public configuration, local examples, runtime secrets, generated state, and ignored files with rotation and scanning evidence.
misconceptions: Adding a secret file to ignore rules repairs prior exposure.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Separate public configuration, local examples, runtime secrets, generated state, and ignored files with rotation and scanning evidence.

competencies.statement: Prove clone-to-gates-to-build behavior in a clean environment and record exact revision, lock, tool, artifact, and failure evidence.
misconceptions: Success from a warm developer directory proves reproducibility.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Prove clone-to-gates-to-build behavior in a clean environment and record exact revision, lock, tool, artifact, and failure evidence.

competencies.statement: Derive architecture from user flows, quality attributes, risk, data sensitivity, team capacity, and change likelihood.
misconceptions: Using a fashionable pattern is an architecture rationale.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Derive architecture from user flows, quality attributes, risk, data sensitivity, team capacity, and change likelihood.

competencies.statement: Separate domain decisions from interface, persistence, network, clock, identity, and third-party effects through narrow owned boundaries.
misconceptions: Creating many folders guarantees useful separation.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Separate domain decisions from interface, persistence, network, clock, identity, and third-party effects through narrow owned boundaries.

competencies.statement: Model identities, states, relationships, invariants, invalid cases, provenance, and lifecycle before choosing storage representation.
misconceptions: A table or interface shape fully defines valid domain behavior.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Model identities, states, relationships, invariants, invalid cases, provenance, and lifecycle before choosing storage representation.

competencies.statement: Specify inputs, runtime validation, authorization, outputs, failures, idempotency, compatibility, and observability at each interface.
misconceptions: Static types validate all external data and operational behavior.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Specify inputs, runtime validation, authorization, outputs, failures, idempotency, compatibility, and observability at each interface.

competencies.statement: Record context, forces, alternatives, decision, consequences, evidence, review trigger, and supersession for consequential choices.
misconceptions: An ADR is permanent justification that should not be revisited.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Record context, forces, alternatives, decision, consequences, evidence, review trigger, and supersession for consequential choices.

competencies.statement: Deliver the smallest accessible end-to-end path through real boundaries with observable build, test, deploy, and recovery signals.
misconceptions: A static mock connected to placeholder data is an operational walking skeleton.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Deliver the smallest accessible end-to-end path through real boundaries with observable build, test, deploy, and recovery signals.

competencies.statement: Implement one domain operation with explicit preconditions, transition, postconditions, failure taxonomy, and stakeholder-visible result.
misconceptions: If output looks correct, internal state transitions need no contract.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Implement one domain operation with explicit preconditions, transition, postconditions, failure taxonomy, and stakeholder-visible result.

competencies.statement: Encode critical invariants at the closest layer and prove a deliberate violation is rejected without corrupting prior state.
misconceptions: Happy-path examples are enough to protect an invariant.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Encode critical invariants at the closest layer and prove a deliberate violation is rejected without corrupting prior state.

competencies.statement: Integrate each slice in a reviewable revision that retrieves prior requirements and leaves the product deployable.
misconceptions: Integration risk can be deferred until all features are complete.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Integrate each slice in a reviewable revision that retrieves prior requirements and leaves the product deployable.

competencies.statement: Refactor only behind behavior, changed-case, accessibility, performance, and failure evidence that separates design change from product change.
misconceptions: Passing type checks proves a refactor preserved behavior.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Refactor only behind behavior, changed-case, accessibility, performance, and failure evidence that separates design change from product change.

competencies.statement: Structure landmarks, headings, names, instructions, controls, errors, and status around the user task and reading order.
misconceptions: ARIA can repair any non-semantic interaction after implementation.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Structure landmarks, headings, names, instructions, controls, errors, and status around the user task and reading order.

competencies.statement: Verify complete keyboard operation, logical order, visible focus, focus movement, escape paths, and no traps across state changes.
misconceptions: Tabbing through a page once proves keyboard accessibility.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Verify complete keyboard operation, logical order, visible focus, focus movement, escape paths, and no traps across state changes.

competencies.statement: Design content and interaction to reflow at zoom and changed mobile, tablet, desktop, orientation, text, and language conditions.
misconceptions: Responsive design means choosing three fixed screen widths.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Design content and interaction to reflow at zoom and changed mobile, tablet, desktop, orientation, text, and language conditions.

competencies.statement: Provide text alternatives, captions, labels, non-color meaning, reduced motion, adequate targets, and tolerant input.
misconceptions: An automated score proves equivalent perception and operation.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Provide text alternatives, captions, labels, non-color meaning, reduced motion, adequate targets, and tolerant input.

competencies.statement: Combine automated checks, keyboard review, screen-reader or structured-text inspection, zoom, contrast, and affected-user evidence with documented limits.
misconceptions: One accessibility tool can certify WCAG conformance.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Combine automated checks, keyboard review, screen-reader or structured-text inspection, zoom, contrast, and affected-user evidence with documented limits.

competencies.statement: Model assets, actors, trust boundaries, misuse, abuse, failure impact, mitigations, detection, response, and residual risk.
misconceptions: Security review is a vulnerability scanner run after coding.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Model assets, actors, trust boundaries, misuse, abuse, failure impact, mitigations, detection, response, and residual risk.

competencies.statement: Trace purpose, collection, consent or authority, use, sharing, retention, access, correction, deletion, and aggregation risk.
misconceptions: Removing direct names makes any dataset anonymous.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Trace purpose, collection, consent or authority, use, sharing, retention, access, correction, deletion, and aggregation risk.

competencies.statement: Assign pure behavior, boundary, integration, interface, end-to-end, accessibility, security, and recovery claims to appropriate tests.
misconceptions: More end-to-end tests provide the strongest and cheapest confidence.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Assign pure behavior, boundary, integration, interface, end-to-end, accessibility, security, and recovery claims to appropriate tests.

competencies.statement: Inject timeout, invalid data, dependency failure, interruption, duplicate action, partial write, and restart conditions and verify safe recovery.
misconceptions: Catching exceptions and showing a message makes a product reliable.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Inject timeout, invalid data, dependency failure, interruption, duplicate action, partial write, and restart conditions and verify safe recovery.

competencies.statement: State which tests passed, what environment they represent, what remains human or production evidence, and who owns each gap.
misconceptions: A green continuous-integration run proves production quality.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: State which tests passed, what environment they represent, what remains human or production evidence, and who owns each gap.

competencies.statement: Run task-based validation against predeclared outcomes and guardrails, analyze success, breakdown, accessibility, and contradictory evidence, then revise.
misconceptions: A satisfaction rating proves task success and user benefit.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Run task-based validation against predeclared outcomes and guardrails, analyze success, breakdown, accessibility, and contradictory evidence, then revise.

competencies.statement: Bind source revision, dependencies, generated assets, build, SBOM or inventory, test evidence, deployment, and rollback to one release identity.
misconceptions: A version tag alone identifies the exact reviewed artifact.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Bind source revision, dependencies, generated assets, build, SBOM or inventory, test evidence, deployment, and rollback to one release identity.

competencies.statement: Define health, logs, metrics, alerts, support, backup, restore, incident, rollback, ownership, and retirement behavior proportionate to risk.
misconceptions: A small portfolio product needs no operational or recovery design.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Define health, logs, metrics, alerts, support, backup, restore, incident, rollback, ownership, and retirement behavior proportionate to risk.

competencies.statement: Create audience-specific setup, architecture, decision, usage, accessibility, security, test, operation, and limitation documentation plus a failure-aware demo.
misconceptions: A polished video can replace runnable and inspectable evidence.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Create audience-specific setup, architecture, decision, usage, accessibility, security, test, operation, and limitation documentation plus a failure-aware demo.

competencies.statement: Defend each mastery and impact claim with traceable evidence, answer counterexamples honestly, reflect on decisions, and define the next changed-context test.
misconceptions: Confidence and presentation polish are substitutes for evidence.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Defend each mastery and impact claim with traceable evidence, answer counterexamples honestly, reflect on decisions, and define the next changed-context test.

credential.title: Independent Product Studio I: Discover, Build, Validate, and Defend Certificate

modules.title: Readiness Audit, Prior-Skill Retrieval, and Evidence Baseline
modules.description: A learner has many completed exercises but cannot show which behaviors are independently reproducible, current, accessible, secure, or useful. Learners cumulatively produce mastery baseline and project readiness ledger through theory, guided work, debugging, transfer, retrieval, and assessment.
objectives: Map prior competencies to observable behaviors, dated artifacts, evidence strength, and unresolved gaps before selecting a product.
Distinguish reusable evidence, practice residue, generated output, sensitive material, and unsupported claims in an existing work sample.
Reproduce one prior behavior under a changed input, environment, or constraint and retain its exact pass or failure evidence.

modules.title: Problem Discovery Before Solution Commitment
modules.description: A community partner asks for an app, but the underlying decision, present workaround, affected users, frequency, severity, and evidence are unknown. Learners cumulatively produce problem evidence dossier through theory, guided work, debugging, transfer, retrieval, and assessment.
objectives: Separate the requested feature from the user need, stakeholder decision, current behavior, and desired outcome.
Observe or reconstruct the current workflow with roles, triggers, inputs, handoffs, delays, exceptions, and consequences.
Compare direct observation, interview, artifact, analytics, support, market, and assumption evidence for relevance and bias.

modules.title: Ethical, Inclusive, and Accessible User Research
modules.description: A team plans convenience-sampled interviews, records unnecessary personal data, and excludes people who use assistive technology or cannot join live video. Learners cumulatively produce consented inclusive research packet through theory, guided work, debugging, transfer, retrieval, and assessment.
objectives: Frame neutral research questions around behavior, context, barriers, and decisions instead of selling a preferred solution.
Recruit relevant variation in ability, device, language, connectivity, role, and problem experience without claiming representativeness.
Explain purpose, recording, storage, withdrawal, incentives, and power relationships in accessible consent before participation.

modules.title: Outcome Contract, Non-Goals, and Measures of Success
modules.description: The proposed product promises engagement and delight but names no user decision, baseline, guardrail, accessibility outcome, or unacceptable harm. Learners cumulatively produce measurable outcome and non-goal contract through theory, guided work, debugging, transfer, retrieval, and assessment.
objectives: Connect product capability to user behavior, user outcome, stakeholder outcome, and public consequence without assuming causality.
Define a baseline, observable leading and lagging measures, collection method, comparison window, and uncertainty.
Pair success measures with accessibility, equity, privacy, security, reliability, and burden guardrails.

modules.title: Hypotheses, Riskiest Assumptions, and Disposable Prototypes
modules.description: A team starts production architecture while desirability, usability, feasibility, viability, safety, and operating assumptions remain untested. Learners cumulatively produce ranked assumption map and prototype evidence through theory, guided work, debugging, transfer, retrieval, and assessment.
objectives: Express desirable, usable, feasible, viable, ethical, accessible, secure, and operable assumptions as falsifiable claims.
Prioritize assumptions by uncertainty, consequence, dependency, and cheapest discriminating evidence.
Choose paper, clickable, coded, data, integration, or operational prototype fidelity to answer one question without production overinvestment.

modules.title: Thin Scope, Vertical Slices, and Evidence-Driven Roadmap
modules.description: A backlog contains many horizontal technical tasks, no complete user path, hidden prerequisites, and no way to demonstrate value until the end. Learners cumulatively produce risk-ordered vertical-slice roadmap through theory, guided work, debugging, transfer, retrieval, and assessment.
objectives: Write a bounded user scenario with trigger, actor, need, outcome, constraints, and observable acceptance evidence.
Slice work across interface, behavior, data, verification, accessibility, security, and operations to produce one end-to-end learning signal.
Order slices by prerequisite knowledge, risk retirement, feedback value, and reversible commitment.

modules.title: Reproducible Repository, Toolchain, and Dependency Stewardship
modules.description: The product runs only on one machine with floating tools, unexplained generated files, unclear license, secrets in examples, and no clean-start proof. Learners cumulatively produce reproducible healthy project repository through theory, guided work, debugging, transfer, retrieval, and assessment.
objectives: Establish purpose, setup, architecture, commands, contribution, conduct, support, security, license, and ownership documentation.
Pin supported runtime, package manager, compiler, formatter, linter, type checker, test runner, and build behavior with compatibility evidence.
Evaluate dependency need, maintenance, provenance, license, advisories, transitive cost, API stability, and replacement or removal path.

modules.title: Small-System Architecture, Data Boundaries, and Decision Records
modules.description: Product logic is coupled to framework, storage, global state, and external services, so changed requirements and tests require broad rewrites. Learners cumulatively produce bounded architecture and data contract packet through theory, guided work, debugging, transfer, retrieval, and assessment.
objectives: Derive architecture from user flows, quality attributes, risk, data sensitivity, team capacity, and change likelihood.
Separate domain decisions from interface, persistence, network, clock, identity, and third-party effects through narrow owned boundaries.
Model identities, states, relationships, invariants, invalid cases, provenance, and lifecycle before choosing storage representation.

modules.title: Walking Skeleton, Core Behavior, and Preserved Invariants
modules.description: A large feature branch contains plausible UI and data but no earlier end-to-end behavior, executable invariant, or safe integration point. Learners cumulatively produce cumulative working product with invariant evidence through theory, guided work, debugging, transfer, retrieval, and assessment.
objectives: Deliver the smallest accessible end-to-end path through real boundaries with observable build, test, deploy, and recovery signals.
Implement one domain operation with explicit preconditions, transition, postconditions, failure taxonomy, and stakeholder-visible result.
Encode critical invariants at the closest layer and prove a deliberate violation is rejected without corrupting prior state.

modules.title: Inclusive Interaction, Content, Responsive Design, and Human Evaluation
modules.description: The primary flow works by mouse at one viewport but loses focus, status, meaning, readability, and error recovery for changed devices and users. Learners cumulatively produce accessible responsive product experience through theory, guided work, debugging, transfer, retrieval, and assessment.
objectives: Structure landmarks, headings, names, instructions, controls, errors, and status around the user task and reading order.
Verify complete keyboard operation, logical order, visible focus, focus movement, escape paths, and no traps across state changes.
Design content and interaction to reflow at zoom and changed mobile, tablet, desktop, orientation, text, and language conditions.

modules.title: Security, Privacy, Reliability, and Layered Quality Evidence
modules.description: A product passes a familiar unit test but trusts input, overcollects data, leaks failure details, loses work, and has no threat or recovery evidence. Learners cumulatively produce trust and quality assurance case through theory, guided work, debugging, transfer, retrieval, and assessment.
objectives: Model assets, actors, trust boundaries, misuse, abuse, failure impact, mitigations, detection, response, and residual risk.
Trace purpose, collection, consent or authority, use, sharing, retention, access, correction, deletion, and aggregation risk.
Assign pure behavior, boundary, integration, interface, end-to-end, accessibility, security, and recovery claims to appropriate tests.

modules.title: Validation, Release, Documentation, Recovery, and Portfolio Defense
modules.description: A demo works from a developer machine, but the audience cannot reproduce it, inspect evidence, understand limits, recover failure, or verify who benefited. Learners cumulatively produce released product evidence and public defense packet through theory, guided work, debugging, transfer, retrieval, and assessment.
objectives: Run task-based validation against predeclared outcomes and guardrails, analyze success, breakdown, accessibility, and contradictory evidence, then revise.
Bind source revision, dependencies, generated assets, build, SBOM or inventory, test evidence, deployment, and rollback to one release identity.
Define health, logs, metrics, alerts, support, backup, restore, incident, rollback, ownership, and retirement behavior proportionate to risk.
